title: Configure quantity-break pricing
goal:
Verify price tiers based on quantity (e.g., 1–9 units at $45,
10–49 at $40, 50+ at $35).

steps.action:
Open FG-BRACKET-A1's pricing. Add quantity breaks:
- 1–9 each: $45.00
- 10–49 each: $40.00
- 50+ each: $35.00
steps.expected: Tiers save with their quantity ranges.

steps.action: Create draft order lines for 5, 25, and 100 units of the part.
steps.expected: Each line uses the right tier price based on quantity.

expected_overall:
Quantity breaks resolve to the correct price tier based on order
quantity.
pass_criteria: All three lines show the expected price per their quantity tier.

title: Configure vendor-specific cost terms for a part
goal:
Set per-vendor cost on a purchased part, including quantity breaks
if applicable. POs default to these costs.

steps.action: Open RM-STEEL-1018-3X3. Find vendor pricing.
steps.expected: A way to add vendor-specific costs is visible.

steps.action:
Add Pacific Steel Supply as a vendor with cost $4.50/foot,
lead time 14 days, MOQ 100 feet.
steps.expected: Vendor pricing record saves.

steps.action: Create a draft PO line for this part to Pacific Steel.
steps.expected: PO line defaults to $4.50/foot.

expected_overall: Per-vendor costs override generic part costs on POs.
pass_criteria:
PO line cost matches the vendor pricing record AND lead time and MOQ
are visible.

title: Create a prototype part marked as not-for-sale
goal:
Verify R&D can create parts marked as prototype (or pre-release) so
they don't accidentally appear on quotes or production schedules.
preconditions: At least one UoM and GL account exist.

steps.action:
Create a part "PROTO-WIDGET-V1" with type: prototype (or a flag
indicating not-for-production / not-for-sale).
steps.expected: Part saves with the prototype indicator.

steps.action: Try to add this part as a line on a sales order.
steps.expected:
The system either blocks selection OR warns the user that this
is a prototype not approved for sale.

expected_overall:
Prototype parts are visibly distinct and gated from sales /
production transactions.
pass_criteria: Prototype indicator visible AND sale of prototype is gated.
why_this_matters:
Real bugs in this area: a salesperson finds a part in search and
quotes it without realizing it's pre-release. The deal closes,
the customer expects shipment, and there's no production-ready BOM.

title: Submit an engineering change request (ECR) and route for approval
goal:
Verify engineering changes go through a formal request, review, and
approval workflow before changing production BOMs/routings.

steps.action:
Find the engineering change request area. Submit a new ECR
against FG-BRACKET-A1 — proposed change: change the bolt size
from M8 to M10. Provide a reason.
steps.expected: ECR saves in "Pending review" status.

steps.action:
Sign in as a different user with approval authority. Open the
ECR queue.
steps.expected: The pending ECR is visible.

steps.action: Approve the ECR.
steps.expected:
ECR status changes to "Approved." A new BOM revision is either
auto-created or a follow-up task is generated.

expected_overall: ECR workflow exists: submit → review → approve → BOM revision.
pass_criteria:
ECR was submitted AND went through approval AND resulted in a
trackable BOM change.

title: Create a prototype tied to a customer feedback record
goal:
When a customer requests a custom variant, the prototype work
should be traceable back to the customer / opportunity that drove it.

steps.action:
Open ACME Industrial's customer record. Find a way to record a
feature request or feedback note.
steps.expected: A field or sub-record for feedback exists.

steps.action: Add a note: "Wants taller bracket variant for new product line."
steps.expected: Note saves with date and originator.

steps.action:
Create prototype part "PROTO-BRACKET-TALL" and link it to the
ACME feedback record.
steps.expected:
Prototype carries a back-link to the originating customer
feedback.

expected_overall:
Customer-driven prototypes are traceable from the customer record
forward to the prototype, and from the prototype back to the customer.
pass_criteria: Bi-directional link exists AND is visible from both ends.
notes:
Customer-feedback-to-prototype traceability is the foundation for
reporting on R&D ROI by customer / segment. Without it, leadership
can't answer "which customers are driving our product roadmap?"

title: Promote a prototype to a production-ready part
goal:
Once a prototype is validated, formally promote it to a production
part. The prototype's BOM and routing become the released versions.
preconditions: The prototype has a BOM and routing.

steps.action:
Open the prototype part. Find the "promote to production" action
(or equivalent — "release," "approve," "graduate").
steps.expected:
The action is available; system prompts for any release info
(revision, effective date, approval signoff).

steps.action: Confirm the promotion.
steps.expected:
Part type changes from prototype to standard. The part is now
sellable and orderable. Original prototype history is preserved.

expected_overall: Prototype is promoted; sellable and orderable as a standard part.
pass_criteria: Type changed AND sellable AND prototype history retained.

title: Capture a sales lead
goal:
Record a new sales opportunity from a webform, trade show, or
inbound call. The lead is not yet a customer.

steps.action:
Find and open the leads area. (Common labels: "Leads,"
"Opportunities," "Prospects.")
steps.expected: The leads list appears.

steps.action:
Create a new lead:
- Company: "Northwest Conveyors Inc."
- Contact: name, email, phone
- Source: "Trade show — IMTS 2026"
- Estimated deal size
- Status: New
steps.expected: Lead saves and appears in the list.

expected_overall: The lead is captured with source attribution.
pass_criteria: Lead exists AND has a source AND has a contact.

title: Qualify a lead and convert it to a customer
goal:
Move a qualified lead through to a real customer record without
losing the contact, source, or activity history.

steps.action: Open the Northwest Conveyors lead. Use the convert action.
steps.expected:
A conversion form appears asking for any extra info needed for
a real customer record (billing terms, credit limit, default tax,
etc.).

steps.action: Fill in the customer-side fields and confirm.
steps.expected:
A new customer record is created. The lead's contact, source,
and notes are carried into the customer record. The lead is
marked converted.

steps.action:
Open the new customer. Verify the lead's history is preserved
and visible.
steps.expected:
Activity from the lead phase (source, notes, dates) is on the
customer record.

expected_overall: Lead-to-customer conversion preserves history.
pass_criteria:
Customer exists AND original lead source visible AND lead notes
preserved.

title: Submit a purchase requisition
goal:
Verify users can request purchases without having direct PO authority.
Requisitions go through approval before becoming a PO.
preconditions: At least one part and one vendor exist.

steps.action:
Find and open the requisitions area. Submit a new requisition:
- Part: RM-STEEL-1018-3X3
- Quantity: 500 ft
- Needed by: 2 weeks from today
- Justification: short text
steps.expected: Requisition saves in "Pending approval" status.

steps.action:
Sign in as a user with PO approval authority. Open the
requisitions queue.
steps.expected: The pending requisition is visible.

steps.action: Approve the requisition.
steps.expected:
Requisition status changes to "Approved." Either auto-creates
a PO or surfaces a "Convert to PO" action.

expected_overall: Requisition workflow exists end-to-end.
pass_criteria: Requisition was submitted AND approved AND linked to a downstream PO.

title: Issue the first purchase order
goal: Create the first real PO against a vendor and submit it.
preconditions: At least one part with vendor pricing exists.

steps.action: Find and open the purchase orders area. Choose to create a new PO.
steps.expected: The PO creation form opens. Required fields are clear.

steps.action:
Select Pacific Steel Supply. Add a line for 500 ft of
RM-STEEL-1018-3X3. Verify default cost ($4.50/ft) and lead time
(14 days) populated automatically from vendor pricing.
steps.expected: Defaults populate correctly. Total amount displays.

steps.action:
Add a second line: 100 each of "Hardware-Bolt-M8" at a plausible
cost. Set ship-to: primary location.
steps.expected: Both lines visible. Order subtotal, tax, and total are calculated.

steps.action: Submit the PO.
steps.expected:
PO transitions to "Issued" or "Sent." It carries a stable PO
number. Status is visible to the vendor (if vendor portal exists)
or via printable PDF.

expected_overall:
First PO issued, with multiple lines, correct defaults, and
available downstream for receipt.
pass_criteria:
PO issued with stable number AND lines reflect entered values
AND it appears in the open-PO list.

title: PO with both inventory and fixed-asset lines
goal:
Verify a single PO can carry both inventory parts (consumed/sold)
and fixed-asset items (capitalized) — and that the GL impact at
receipt is correct for each line type.
preconditions: Asset GL account exists from P1.

steps.action:
Create a new PO to a vendor that supplies both. Add:
- Line 1: Inventory — 100 ft of RM-STEEL-1018-3X3
- Line 2: Fixed asset — 1 each of "Hydraulic Press 60-ton" at
  $35,000 (asset class, capitalize)
steps.expected:
Form differentiates the two. Fixed-asset line requires asset
class, capitalization GL account, and an estimated useful life.

steps.action: Submit the PO.
steps.expected: PO issued. Both line types visible.

expected_overall:
PO supports mixed line types. Distinction persists for downstream
receipt processing.
pass_criteria:
Both lines submitted on one PO AND each line carries its own
account/class designation.
why_this_matters:
ERPs that don't support mixed POs force buyers to issue separate
POs to the same vendor for the same delivery — that's friction
that real shops don't accept.

title: Amend a PO after issuance, before receipt
goal:
Verify POs can be revised (quantity change, price update) and the
vendor is notified, with a clear amendment trail.
preconditions: P3-PO-001 has passed (an issued PO exists).

steps.action: Open the issued PO. Use the amend or revise action.
steps.expected:
The PO becomes editable in revision mode. The original is
preserved.

steps.action: Increase line 1 quantity from 500 to 750 ft. Save.
steps.expected:
Amendment saves. PO version increments. Revision history
visible.

steps.action: Re-send to vendor (if there's a "send" action).
steps.expected:
Vendor receives the amended PO; original is superseded but
preserved for audit.

expected_overall: PO amendments are tracked. Vendors can see what changed.
pass_criteria:
Old version preserved AND new version active AND change history
visible.

title: Receive against a PO at the dock
goal:
Record a vendor receipt — material physically arrived. Inventory
comes into the system; the PO line moves to received status.

steps.action:
Find and open the receiving area. Either scan the PO barcode
or search by PO number.
steps.expected:
The PO opens. Receivable lines are visible with their
ordered/received/remaining quantities.

steps.action:
Receive line 1: 500 ft of RM-STEEL-1018-3X3. Enter lot number
"PSS-2026-04-001" (since the part is lot-tracked from P2-PART-003).
steps.expected:
Lot prompt appears (because the part is lot-tracked). Lot is
recorded.

steps.action: Receive line 2: 100 each of Hardware-Bolt-M8.
steps.expected: No lot prompt for non-lot-tracked part. Quantity received.

steps.action: Confirm and post the receipt.
steps.expected:
Inventory increases. PO status changes to "Received" or
"Closed." A receipt document is created and printable.

expected_overall:
First inventory exists. PO is closed. GL impact: inventory account
debited, GR/IR (goods received / invoice received) account credited.
pass_criteria:
Inventory increased by received quantity AND lot recorded for
lot-tracked items AND PO marked received.

negative_variants.title: Try to receive more than ordered
negative_variants.action:
On the same PO, try to enter receipt quantity 600 (greater
than 500 ordered).
negative_variants.expected:
The system warns or blocks the over-receipt. If allowed (with
tolerance), it must be flagged for review.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Over-receipt was either blocked OR flagged AND not silently accepted.

negative_variants.title: Try to receive without lot number on lot-tracked part
negative_variants.action: Try to receive line 1 without entering a lot number.
negative_variants.expected: The system blocks submission and explains that a lot is required.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Submission blocked AND error names the lot field.

title: Receive a partial shipment
goal:
Verify the system correctly handles a partial receipt — line stays
open, PO stays open, vendor expected to ship the balance.
preconditions: An issued PO exists with at least one line.

steps.action:
Open a PO with an unreceived line for 100 each of a part.
Receive only 60 of them.
steps.expected: Line accepts partial receipt. Remaining quantity (40) is visible.

steps.action: Save / post the receipt.
steps.expected:
Inventory increased by 60. PO line shows 60 received, 40 on order.
PO is still open.

expected_overall:
Partial receipts are supported. PO stays open until fully received
or short-closed.
pass_criteria:
Inventory matches partial quantity AND PO line still open AND
remaining quantity correct.

title: Receive partially with quality rejection
goal:
Verify that received material can be split between accepted and
rejected at the dock, with the rejected quantity returned or
isolated for quality review.
preconditions: An issued PO with a line for 500 ft of steel.

steps.action:
At receipt, mark 50 ft as "damaged on arrival" while accepting
the remaining 450 ft.
steps.expected:
The system supports splitting the receipt — accepted vs.
rejected. Rejected goes to a quarantine bin / status, NOT into
regular inventory.

steps.action: Initiate a return-to-vendor for the rejected 50 ft.
steps.expected:
A return-to-vendor (RTV) document is created. The rejected
material is isolated until the RTV ships.

expected_overall:
Receipt-time quality issues are handled in-line, not as a
follow-up workflow.
pass_criteria:
Accepted quantity in regular inventory AND rejected quantity
isolated AND RTV document created.

title: Commission a fixed asset received on a PO
goal:
When a fixed-asset PO line is received, the asset record is either
auto-created or surfaces for an authoritative final commissioning
step (asset tag, location, in-service date).
preconditions: P3-PO-002 has passed (PO with a fixed-asset line).
P3-RECV-001 or equivalent has received the asset line.

steps.action:
Open the asset commissioning queue (or the fixed-asset side of
the receipt). The "Hydraulic Press 60-ton" should be visible
pending commissioning.
steps.expected: A commissioning task or draft asset record exists.

steps.action:
Complete commissioning: assign asset tag, location: primary
location, work center: Press Shop, in-service date: today,
depreciation method: straight-line, useful life: 10 years.
steps.expected: Asset record finalizes. Depreciation schedule is generated.

steps.action:
Verify GL impact: $35,000 debit to fixed-asset capitalization
account, credit to GR/IR. A vendor invoice received later will
clear GR/IR.
steps.expected: GL postings reflect the capitalization (no expense hit).

expected_overall:
Fixed asset is on the books with depreciation running. GL impact
is correct (capitalization, not expense).
pass_criteria:
Asset record exists AND depreciation schedule visible AND GL
postings reflect capitalization.
why_this_matters:
This is where many ERPs leak the inventory-vs-asset distinction.
If the receipt posts to inventory by mistake, the controller is
doing journal-entry corrections at month-end. Catching this at
commissioning time saves real time.

title: 3-way match a vendor invoice to PO and receipt
goal:
Verify the standard 3-way match — invoice from vendor matches the
PO (price) and the receipt (quantity) before payment is approved.

steps.action:
Find the AP invoice entry area. Enter a vendor invoice from
Pacific Steel Supply: 500 ft at $4.50/ft, total $2,250.
steps.expected: Form prompts for PO/receipt matching.

steps.action: Match against the PO and receipt from earlier.
steps.expected:
The system shows that price ($4.50/ft = PO), quantity
(500 ft = receipt), and total ($2,250) all match. Invoice
is ready for approval.

steps.action:
Approve the invoice for payment. Verify GL impact: GR/IR
cleared, AP credited.
steps.expected: AP increases. GR/IR clears. Invoice scheduled per terms.

expected_overall: 3-way match works correctly. GR/IR clears at invoice receipt.
pass_criteria: Match passed AND AP entry posted AND GR/IR account cleared.

negative_variants.title: Invoice with price mismatch
negative_variants.action: Enter a vendor invoice with a price of $5.00/ft (PO had $4.50).
negative_variants.expected:
System flags the price mismatch. Either blocks approval or
routes to controller for tolerance approval.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Mismatch flagged AND requires extra approval to proceed.

title: Enter opening inventory balances from a prior system
goal:
When migrating from another system (or from spreadsheets), enter
starting inventory quantities and unit costs. The system creates
the opening GL postings to balance.
preconditions: Parts exist for at least 5 items.
Inventory and equity GL accounts exist.

steps.action:
Find the opening balances area or "physical count entry" /
"starting inventory."
steps.expected:
A way to enter opening balances exists, distinct from regular
inventory adjustments.

steps.action:
Enter quantities and unit costs for 5 parts. Set the as-of date
to today (or the migration cutover date).
steps.expected:
Each entry accepts quantity, unit cost, and lot number where
applicable. Total inventory value is calculated.

steps.action: Post the opening balances.
steps.expected:
Inventory exists for each part. Equivalent equity / opening-balance
GL entry is auto-created so the books balance.

expected_overall:
Opening balances are posted. Inventory matches expectations. Books
are in balance.
pass_criteria:
All 5 parts have correct opening quantity AND a corresponding
equity GL entry exists.

title: Enter opening AR and AP balances
goal:
Bring open invoices and open vendor invoices over from a prior
system as opening balances, properly aged.
preconditions: At least one customer and one vendor exist.

steps.action:
Open the AR opening balance area. Enter:
- Customer: ACME Industrial
- Original invoice date: 30 days ago
- Amount: $15,000
- Due date: today
steps.expected: AR entry is created with correct aging.

steps.action:
Open the AP opening balance area. Enter a similar prior-period
open vendor invoice for $5,000.
steps.expected: AP entry is created with correct aging.

steps.action: Run an AR aging report and AP aging report.
steps.expected: Both opening balances appear in the correct aging buckets.

expected_overall:
Opening AR and AP balances are entered with correct aging and
appear in standard reports.
pass_criteria: AR and AP totals match what was entered AND aging is correct.

title: Run a cycle count on opening inventory
goal:
Verify the cycle count workflow: generate a count list, count
physically, enter results, reconcile any variance.

steps.action:
Find and open the cycle count area. Generate a count for 5
randomly selected parts (or a specific bin / location).
steps.expected:
Count list created with expected quantities hidden (so the
counter doesn't bias toward the system value).

steps.action:
Enter physical count quantities. For 4 parts, enter the system
quantity. For 1 part, enter a quantity 5 less than system
(simulating a variance).
steps.expected: Counts are recorded. Variance for the 5th part is flagged.

steps.action: Review the variance. Approve the adjustment.
steps.expected:
Inventory adjustment posts. GL impact: inventory account
adjusted by variance value, offset to inventory variance /
shrinkage account.

expected_overall:
Cycle count complete with variance properly recorded and GL impact
correct.
pass_criteria: Counts entered AND variance flagged AND adjustment posted.

title: Move inventory between bins after count
goal:
Verify bin-level inventory movement records correctly without
changing total quantity on hand.
preconditions: Inventory in at least one bin.

steps.action:
Find the bin transfer area. Move 50 ft of RM-STEEL-1018-3X3
from bin A to bin B.
steps.expected: Transfer accepts source bin, destination bin, quantity.

steps.action: Confirm the transfer.
steps.expected:
Bin A quantity decreases by 50; bin B quantity increases by 50.
Total on-hand is unchanged. No GL impact.

expected_overall: Bin transfers move inventory location without changing valuation.
pass_criteria:
Source quantity decreased AND dest quantity increased AND total
unchanged AND no GL postings.

title: Pay an approved vendor invoice
goal:
Verify the payment workflow — issue a check or ACH for an
approved invoice, post the payment, and clear the AP entry.
preconditions: P3-AP-001 has passed (an approved AP invoice exists).

steps.action:
Open the AP payments area. Select the approved Pacific Steel
Supply invoice.
steps.expected: The invoice appears with full amount due.

steps.action: Issue payment by check (or ACH if integrated). Confirm.
steps.expected:
Payment is recorded. Check number / ACH ref is captured.
Invoice is marked paid.

steps.action: Verify GL impact: cash credited, AP debited (cleared).
steps.expected:
GL postings are correct. AP balance for this vendor decreases
by the payment amount.

expected_overall: Vendor payment processed end-to-end. AP cleared.
pass_criteria: Invoice marked paid AND cash and AP postings are correct.

title: Create a quote for a customer
goal:
Build a quote for a customer — pricing, lead time, terms — that
can be accepted and converted to a sales order.
preconditions: At least one finished part with pricing (P2-PART-002, P2-PRICE-001).

steps.action: Find and open the quotes area. Choose to create a new quote.
steps.expected: Quote creation form opens.

steps.action:
Select ACME Industrial as the customer. Add a line: 100 each of
FG-BRACKET-A1.
steps.expected:
Default unit price is $35.00 (Distributor Pricing list from
P2-PRICE-001), not the part's $45 list.

steps.action:
Verify lead time defaults from the part's master data. Add a
validity period (e.g., quote good for 30 days). Save.
steps.expected:
Quote saves with a stable quote number, all calculated totals
visible, and validity dates set.

steps.action:
Send the quote to the customer (email, PDF download, or whatever
the system supports).
steps.expected: Quote is delivered. Status changes to "Sent."

expected_overall:
First quote exists, with correct customer pricing applied, and is
in the customer's hands.
pass_criteria: Quote saved AND priced from the right list AND sent to the customer.

title: Override default pricing on a quote line
goal:
Verify a salesperson can override default pricing for a one-off
deal, with the override flagged for visibility.

steps.action:
Create a new quote for ACME with a line for 200 units of
FG-BRACKET-A1. Override the unit price from $35 to $30 (deeper
discount).
steps.expected:
Override is accepted with a clear visual indicator showing
the price was overridden.

steps.action: Save and view the quote.
steps.expected:
Override is visible. Margin or markup analysis (if available)
shows the impact.

expected_overall: Pricing overrides are supported and surfaced clearly.
pass_criteria:
Override visible AND price reflected on the quote AND override
flag visible to reviewers.

title: Convert an accepted quote to a sales order
goal:
Move from quote to sales order — the customer agreed, now we
commit production and delivery.

steps.action: Open the quote. Use the convert-to-SO action.
steps.expected:
A new sales order is created with all quote lines, prices, and
terms carried over.

steps.action:
Add or confirm the customer PO number, requested ship date,
and ship-to address.
steps.expected: All values save.

steps.action: Submit / confirm the SO.
steps.expected:
SO transitions to "Open" or "Booked." Quote is marked as
converted.

expected_overall: SO exists and references the original quote for traceability.
pass_criteria:
SO is open AND links to the original quote AND carries all the
quote's pricing.

title: Release a work order from a sales order
goal:
Generate and release a work order to fulfill the SO. The WO carries
the BOM and routing from the part's master data and is assigned to
the appropriate work centers.
preconditions: The part on the SO has a BOM and routing.

steps.action:
Open the sales order. Use the "release to production" or
"create work order" action.
steps.expected:
Work order is generated. WO number, target quantity, BOM
revision, and routing are all populated.

steps.action:
Verify the operations match the part's routing — Cut, Weld,
Paint, Inspect at the right work centers.
steps.expected:
All operations are present in the right order with the work
centers from P2-ROUTE-001.

steps.action: Release the WO to production.
steps.expected:
WO status changes from "Planned" to "Released" (or equivalent).
It now appears on the floor's work-to-do list.

expected_overall:
Work order is on the floor, with BOM and routing locked at the
revision active when released.
pass_criteria:
WO released AND links to its source SO AND its BOM/routing
revisions match what was active at release.

title: Verify the work order appears on the kanban / sprint board
goal:
When a WO is released, it should show up as a card on the
kanban-style task board so the production team can see and pick
it up.

steps.action: Open the kanban / task board for the production area.
steps.expected:
The newly released WO appears as a card in the appropriate
"Ready" or "Released" column.

steps.action: Open the card.
steps.expected:
Card shows: WO number, part, target quantity, due date, current
operation, work center.

expected_overall: Released WOs are surfaced as kanban cards for the floor to pick up.
pass_criteria:
WO is visible on the board AND card has identifying details AND
state matches the WO's released status.

title: Start the first operation on a released work order
goal:
Begin work at the floor: scan or pick the WO, identify the work
center, and transition the operation from Released to In Progress.

steps.action:
At the work center's station (paired scanner workstation, or
mobile device). Sign in or scan badge. Scan the WO ID. Scan
the start barcode (or tap the start action).
steps.expected:
The combination of barcodes resolves: this WO, this work
center, this operator, start. Operation transitions to
"In Progress."

steps.action: Verify the kanban card has moved from "Ready" to "In Progress."
steps.expected:
The card moved automatically without anyone touching the kanban
UI.

steps.action:
Verify labor time is now being tracked against the WO and
operator.
steps.expected: A labor entry is open and accumulating against the operator.

expected_overall: Operation is in progress. Labor is tracking. Kanban reflects status.
pass_criteria:
Status changed AND operator recorded AND labor time accumulating
AND kanban moved.

title: Issue material from inventory to the work order
goal:
Consume the BOM components from inventory and post them against the
WO so material variance can be tracked.
preconditions: Inventory exists for the BOM components (from P3 receipt).

steps.action:
At the floor station, scan the WO. Choose to issue material.
The system shows the BOM and required quantities.
steps.expected:
For each BOM line, expected quantity is shown. Where lots are
required, a lot prompt appears.

steps.action:
Issue the steel: scan the lot "PSS-2026-04-001" and confirm
the required quantity (50 ft for 100 units).
steps.expected:
Lot is recorded against the WO. Inventory decreases by the
issued quantity.

steps.action: Issue the bolts and washers (no lots required).
steps.expected: Inventory decreases. WO material consumption is updated.

expected_overall:
Material is consumed from inventory and traced against the WO,
including lot traceability for lot-tracked parts.
pass_criteria:
Inventory decreased correctly AND lot trace recorded AND WO shows
material issued matches BOM.

negative_variants.title: Insufficient inventory to issue
negative_variants.action: Try to issue a quantity greater than what's available in stock.
negative_variants.expected:
System blocks the issue or warns. Either offers an alternate
lot, requires a backorder, or refuses to consume more than
on-hand.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Issue blocked or warned AND inventory not over-issued.

title: Record labor time against the operation
goal:
Record actual labor time against the work order operation, so
labor cost variance can be calculated against the routing's
standard.

steps.action:
At the operation's completion (or at break / end of shift), use
the scanner or manual entry to clock out of this operation.
steps.expected:
Labor entry closes with start and end timestamps. Total time
is calculated.

steps.action:
Verify the labor cost is calculated using the work center's
default labor rate (from P1-WC-001).
steps.expected: Labor cost shows the rate × time. WO actual labor cost increases.

expected_overall: Labor time and cost are tracked against the WO and operation.
pass_criteria:
Labor entry closed AND cost calculated AND WO labor cost increased
by the entry.

title: Complete the operation and move to the next
goal:
Mark the operation done — produce the expected quantity of good
pieces, optionally record scrap, and trigger the next operation.

steps.action:
Scan the WO and the complete-operation action. Enter:
- Good quantity: 98 (out of target 100)
- Scrap quantity: 2
- Scrap reason: "Setup miscut" (or similar)
steps.expected:
System accepts. Operation transitions to "Complete." Next
operation in routing transitions to "Ready."

steps.action:
Verify the kanban card moves from "In Progress" to "Ready"
for the next work center.
steps.expected:
Card moved. The work center shown on the card is the next in
routing.

expected_overall:
Operation is complete with good and scrap recorded. Next operation
is ready.
pass_criteria:
Quantities recorded AND scrap reason captured AND next operation
ready.

title: Complete the final operation and move WO to inventory
goal:
When the last routing operation completes, finished goods enter
available inventory. If the part is serial-tracked, serial numbers
are generated.
preconditions: All preceding operations on the WO have completed.
WO is on the final inspection or final operation.

steps.action:
Complete the final operation (Inspection). Enter good quantity:
98.
steps.expected:
WO is closed. Finished goods inventory increases by 98 units.
If part is serial-tracked, prompt for serial numbers.

steps.action:
Verify the GL impact: WIP credited, finished goods inventory
debited, with cost based on standard or actual depending on
costing model.
steps.expected: GL postings are correct.

expected_overall: Finished goods are on the shelf. WO is closed. WIP is cleared.
pass_criteria: FG inventory increased AND WIP cleared AND any serials recorded.

title: Move finished goods from the staging area to a bin
goal:
After WO completion, physically move the goods to a storage bin
and record the move.

steps.action:
In the put-away area, scan the part / WO label. Scan or select
the destination bin (e.g., "FG-BIN-A1").
steps.expected: System records the move. Inventory location updates.

steps.action: Verify the part is now allocatable from that bin.
steps.expected: Available-to-pick view shows the goods in the new bin.

expected_overall:
Finished goods are at their storage location and available for
pick.
pass_criteria: Goods at correct bin AND available for downstream allocation.

title: Pick inventory for the sales order
goal:
Allocate finished goods to the SO and pick them physically — the
start of the shipping side of the cycle.

steps.action: Open the pick queue or the SO. Generate a pick list.
steps.expected:
Pick list shows: SO number, customer, items, quantities, bin
locations. If serials are required, the list includes serials
to scan during pick.

steps.action:
Pick at the bin: scan the part, confirm quantity (and serials
if applicable).
steps.expected: Each pick scan reduces bin quantity. Pick list updates.

steps.action: Mark the pick complete.
steps.expected:
Goods are staged for shipping. SO line is allocated and ready
to ship.

expected_overall: Pick is complete. Goods are at the staging area, ready to ship.
pass_criteria:
All pick lines complete AND inventory location updated AND SO
allocated.

title: Pack the order with proper documentation
goal:
Pack picked goods into shipping containers with packing slip and
any customer-specific labeling requirements.

steps.action:
Open the pack area. Pull up the staged SO. Scan items into a
shipping container.
steps.expected:
Each item is verified against the SO line. Mismatches are
flagged.

steps.action: Generate a packing slip and any required labels.
steps.expected:
Documents print or download. They include SO number, customer
PO, line items, and any special instructions.

expected_overall: Order is packed and documented, ready to hand off to the carrier.
pass_criteria: Packing complete AND packing slip generated AND items match the SO.

title: Ship the order
goal:
Hand off the packed order to a carrier — domestic or international —
capturing tracking information and triggering the customer invoice.
preconditions: A shipping integration is configured (P0-INTEG-002) — or manual shipment entry.

steps.action:
Open the ship area. Select the packed order. Choose a carrier
and service level (or use the default).
steps.expected:
System produces a shipping label and tracking number — either
via carrier integration or manual entry.

steps.action: Confirm the ship.
steps.expected:
SO marks as shipped. Tracking number is recorded on the SO.
Inventory clears the staging area. The customer is (optionally)
notified.

steps.action:
Verify the next step: an invoice is either auto-generated or
surfaces in the AR queue.
steps.expected: Invoice is created from the shipped SO.

expected_overall:
Shipment is on its way. SO is shipped. Customer invoice is queued
or created.
pass_criteria: Shipment created AND tracking number recorded AND invoice triggered.

title: Ship internationally with customs documentation
goal:
Verify international shipments produce the right customs paperwork
(commercial invoice, harmonized codes, country-of-origin).
preconditions: That customer has an SO ready to ship.

steps.action:
In the ship workflow for the Munich Manufacturing GmbH order,
verify the customs documentation prompts appear.
steps.expected:
Form requests harmonized tariff codes, country of origin,
commercial-invoice value, and incoterm.

steps.action: Fill in the required fields and confirm.
steps.expected:
Commercial invoice and any other required documents (e.g.,
certificate of origin) are generated.

expected_overall: International shipments produce complete customs paperwork.
pass_criteria:
Commercial invoice generated AND harmonized codes captured AND
incoterm recorded.

title: Generate the customer invoice
goal:
Bill the customer for the shipped goods, with correct line items,
taxes, and terms.

steps.action:
Open the AR / invoicing queue. The shipped SO appears as
ready to invoice.
steps.expected:
Invoice draft pre-populated with SO data: lines, prices, tax,
terms, ship-to.

steps.action:
Review the draft. Confirm the customer PO number is on the
invoice. Verify tax calculation matches expectations for the
ship-to jurisdiction.
steps.expected: Tax is calculated correctly based on customer's tax code.

steps.action: Post the invoice.
steps.expected:
AR balance for ACME increases. GL impact: AR debited, revenue
credited, sales tax payable credited.

steps.action: Send the invoice to the customer (email, PDF, EDI).
steps.expected: Invoice is delivered. Status changes to "Sent."

expected_overall: Customer invoice is in the customer's hands and on the AR ledger.
pass_criteria:
Invoice posted AND AR increased AND invoice sent AND GL postings
correct.

title: Apply customer payment to the invoice
goal:
Receive payment from the customer and apply it to the open
invoice — closing out the cycle.

steps.action:
Find the cash receipts area. Enter:
- Customer: ACME Industrial
- Payment amount: full invoice total
- Method: check, ACH, or wire
- Check / reference number
steps.expected: Form accepts the values.

steps.action: Apply the payment to the open invoice from P4-INV-001.
steps.expected:
System matches the payment to the invoice. Invoice status
changes to "Paid."

steps.action: Verify GL impact: cash debited, AR credited.
steps.expected: GL postings are correct.

expected_overall:
Quote-to-cash cycle complete. Invoice paid. Cycle profitability
can now be analyzed.
pass_criteria:
Payment applied AND invoice marked paid AND GL postings correct
AND AR balance for customer reduced.

title: Apply a partial payment
goal:
Verify partial payments are supported — invoice stays open with
reduced balance.

steps.action: Receive a partial payment (e.g., 50% of an invoice). Apply.
steps.expected: Invoice balance reduces. Invoice stays open with remainder.

steps.action:
Verify aging is preserved (if invoice was 25 days old, it's
still 25 days old, not reset to 0).
steps.expected: Aging is correct. Customer statement shows the partial.

expected_overall: Partial payments work. Aging is preserved.
pass_criteria: Balance reduced by payment AND invoice still open AND aging unchanged.

title: Apply an overpayment as customer credit
goal:
Handle when a customer pays more than the invoice — the difference
becomes credit on file, applicable to future invoices.

steps.action: Receive a payment greater than the invoice total. Apply.
steps.expected: Invoice closes. Excess shows as customer credit / unapplied cash.

steps.action: Open the customer record.
steps.expected: Available credit is visible.

expected_overall:
Overpayments produce customer credit, not refunds (unless
explicitly chosen).
pass_criteria: Invoice closed AND credit recorded AND visible on customer record.

title: Onboard a new employee end-to-end
goal:
Hire a new floor operator: complete employment paperwork (W-4,
I-9, direct deposit, background check, drug test status), assign
pay info, location, and trigger the rest.
preconditions: At least one location and pay structure exist (P1).

steps.action:
Find the new-hire workflow. Start onboarding for a new
employee:
- Name, address, contact info
- Hire date: today
- Pay: hourly, $22/hr, non-exempt, eligible for OT
- Location: primary
- Department: Production
steps.expected: Onboarding form accepts all values.

steps.action:
Capture or upload required documents:
- W-4 (federal withholding)
- I-9 (work authorization)
- Direct deposit info
- Background check cleared
- Drug test cleared
steps.expected:
Each document is recorded with date, status, and an audit
trail. Required documents are gated — onboarding can't complete
without them.

steps.action: Capture digital signatures where required.
steps.expected: Signatures are captured and stored with the document.

expected_overall:
New employee record is complete. Required compliance documentation
is in place. Employee is ready for system access and first
assignment.
pass_criteria:
Employee record exists AND all required documents present with
signatures AND no compliance gates open.
why_this_matters:
Missing I-9 paperwork on day-1 is a real fineable offense. The
system needs to be the source of truth for this, not a folder of
scanned PDFs.

title: Grant system access to the new hire
goal:
Create a system user for the new employee, link them, and assign
the appropriate role.

steps.action: Open the new employee record. Initiate user creation.
steps.expected: A new user is created or invited via email.

steps.action: Assign the Floor Operator role.
steps.expected:
Role is assigned. User has the right permissions for floor
work.

steps.action:
Have the new hire sign in. Verify they see the floor app and
not administrative areas.
steps.expected: Role-appropriate UI is visible. Restricted areas are not.

expected_overall: New hire has system access. Their first sign-in shows the right UI.
pass_criteria:
User created AND linked to employee AND role assigned AND access
appropriately scoped.

title: Assign a work order operation to the new hire
goal:
Put the new hire on their first task — a real WO operation at a
work center where they're trained.
preconditions: At least one open WO operation exists.

steps.action:
As a planner, open the kanban board. Drag (or assign) a
released operation to the new hire.
steps.expected: Assignment saves. The card is now tagged to the new hire.

steps.action: Sign in as the new hire. Open their assigned work list.
steps.expected: The assignment is visible. They can scan the WO and start work.

steps.action:
Verify their training records (if tracked) match the work
center / operation. If not trained, the system should warn.
steps.expected:
Either: they're trained and start; or: a training warning is
surfaced.

expected_overall: New hire is on their first task, with training validation if applicable.
pass_criteria:
Assignment visible to the operator AND any required training is
validated or flagged.

title: Report damage to a piece of equipment
goal:
Floor operator notices damage on equipment (a press hydraulic leak,
a worn fixture) and logs it. A maintenance ticket is created.
preconditions: At least one fixed asset record (P1-ASSET-001).

steps.action:
At the press, scan the asset tag. Choose "Report damage" from
the asset's actions.
steps.expected: A damage report form opens, pre-filled with the asset.

steps.action:
Enter:
- Description: "Hydraulic fluid leak from main cylinder"
- Severity: Medium (operable but degraded)
- Photos (if supported): one or two
steps.expected: Form accepts. Severity drives downstream priority.

steps.action: Submit. Verify a maintenance ticket / work order is created.
steps.expected:
A maintenance WO appears in the maintenance queue, linked to
the asset.

expected_overall: Damage is logged, photographed, and dispatched to maintenance.
pass_criteria:
Maintenance WO created AND linked to the asset AND visible in the
maintenance queue.

title: Maintenance triages a damage report and assigns a tech
goal:
The maintenance manager reviews the damage report, sets priority,
and assigns a tech. The kanban / scheduling board reflects this.

steps.action: Open the maintenance queue. Find the new ticket.
steps.expected:
Ticket visible with severity, asset, description, and
reporter.

steps.action:
Set priority: High (blocks production). Assign to a tech.
Schedule for today.
steps.expected:
Ticket updates with assignment. The tech sees it on their
dashboard.

expected_overall: Ticket triaged and dispatched.
pass_criteria: Priority set AND tech assigned AND tech sees the ticket.

title: Maintenance tech executes the repair
goal:
Tech does the work, consumes any parts, records time, and closes
the ticket.

steps.action: Open the assigned ticket. Scan or click "Start work."
steps.expected: Time tracking begins.

steps.action:
Issue any required parts (from inventory, e.g., a hydraulic
fitting and a quart of fluid). Record consumed quantities.
steps.expected: Inventory decreases. Parts cost is recorded against the ticket.

steps.action:
Add a completion note: what was done, root cause if known,
recommended preventative action.
steps.expected: Note saves with timestamp.

steps.action: Close the ticket.
steps.expected:
Ticket closed. Asset returned to operable status. Total cost
(labor + parts) recorded against the asset's lifetime cost.

expected_overall: Repair is complete and costed. Asset is back to operable.
pass_criteria: Ticket closed AND parts and labor recorded AND asset back online.

title: Configure a preventative maintenance schedule
goal:
Define a PM schedule on a critical asset — every N hours of run
time, or every N days, or both — so the system can trigger
preventative work before damage happens.

steps.action: Open the press asset. Find the PM section.
steps.expected: A way to define PM schedules is present.

steps.action:
Add a schedule:
- Type: Quarterly (every 90 days)
- Description: "Hydraulic fluid change + filter inspection"
- Required parts: hydraulic fluid, filter
- Estimated labor: 2 hours
steps.expected: Schedule saves.

steps.action:
Set the next due date and verify the system will create a PM WO
automatically when due.
steps.expected:
Next-due date is visible. A scheduled job will create the WO
when the date arrives.

expected_overall:
PM schedule is configured. The system will trigger work before
damage occurs.
pass_criteria:
Schedule exists AND next-due is visible AND scheduled trigger
is in place.

title: Trigger a PM work order manually and complete it
goal:
Verify the manual trigger path for PMs (e.g., advancing schedule
for a planned shutdown) and that execution mirrors damage repair.

steps.action: From the press's PM section, manually trigger the next PM.
steps.expected:
A PM work order is created and assigned (or queued for
assignment).

steps.action:
As the assigned tech, execute the PM: parts, labor, completion
note.
steps.expected:
PM completes. Schedule advances — next due date moves forward
by the configured interval.

expected_overall: PM cycle is closed. Schedule advances automatically.
pass_criteria: PM WO closed AND parts/labor recorded AND next-due date advanced.

title: A QC inspection fails — initiate rework
goal:
At a QC step in a production routing, parts fail inspection. They
loop back through the routing for rework rather than going to
finished goods.
preconditions: A WO is at an inspection operation.

steps.action:
At inspection, scan the parts. Mark 3 of the 100 as failed.
Reason: "Weld porosity."
steps.expected:
Failure is recorded. The 3 parts move to a rework status, not
to finished goods.

steps.action: Choose the rework path: send back to Weld for repair.
steps.expected:
System creates a rework operation back at Weld for the 3 units.
The original WO continues with 97 units still moving forward.

steps.action: Verify the rework operation is visible to the Weld work center.
steps.expected: Rework is on Weld's queue.

expected_overall:
QC failures route to rework with full traceability of which units
were reworked at which operations.
pass_criteria:
Failed units flagged AND routed to rework AND traceable AND main
WO continues for the good units.

title: Customer initiates a return (RMA)
goal:
Customer reports a problem with a shipped product. RMA is created
and the customer is told how to ship it back.
preconditions: At least one shipped invoice exists (P4-SHIP / P4-INV-001).

steps.action:
Find the RMAs area. Create a new RMA referencing the original
invoice / order.
steps.expected:
RMA form opens. Lines and prices pre-fill from the original
shipment.

steps.action:
Fill in: returned quantity, reason ("dimensional out-of-spec"),
desired resolution (replace / repair / refund).
steps.expected: RMA saves with a stable number.

steps.action:
Generate an RMA confirmation for the customer with shipping
instructions.
steps.expected:
Confirmation includes the RMA number, return address, and any
shipping label or instructions.

expected_overall: RMA is created and the customer has return instructions.
pass_criteria:
RMA exists AND has a stable number AND the customer document
includes return shipping instructions.

title: Receive returned product
goal: Physically receive the returned product, inspect, and route it.

steps.action: At receiving, scan the RMA number on the inbound package.
steps.expected: The expected RMA opens.

steps.action: Receive the returned units. Inspect them.
steps.expected:
Each unit is recorded as received. QC outcome can be set per
unit (return-to-stock, rework, scrap).

steps.action: Route 5 units to scrap (unsalvageable), 5 to rework.
steps.expected:
Scrap and rework destinations are set. Inventory updates
accordingly (no scrap units back to FG inventory).

expected_overall:
Returned product is in the right disposition: scrap, rework, or
return-to-stock based on inspection.
pass_criteria:
All units accounted for AND each routed to its disposition AND
inventory not contaminated with bad stock.

title: Issue a credit memo against the returned invoice
goal:
Settle the financial side of the RMA — credit the customer for the
returned units, reducing AR.

steps.action:
Create a credit memo from the RMA. It pre-fills with the
returned quantities and prices.
steps.expected: Credit memo draft appears.

steps.action: Confirm and post.
steps.expected:
Credit memo posts. AR decreases. GL impact: revenue reduced,
AR credited.

steps.action:
Apply the credit memo to the original invoice (or, if invoice
is paid, leave as customer credit).
steps.expected: Credit memo applied appropriately.

expected_overall: Customer's account is settled.
pass_criteria:
Credit memo posted AND applied or held as credit AND AR/revenue
postings correct.

title: Return defective material to a vendor
goal:
Process a return-to-vendor (RTV) for material rejected on receipt
or discovered defective in production.
preconditions: Some quarantined or defective material exists from P3-RECV-003 or similar.

steps.action:
Find the RTV area. Create an RTV against Pacific Steel Supply
for 50 ft of defective steel.
steps.expected: RTV draft is created.

steps.action:
Enter reason and submit. Generate vendor-facing documentation
(RMA-equivalent for the vendor).
steps.expected: RTV document is ready to attach to the shipment back.

steps.action:
Once shipped, mark the RTV as shipped. Quarantined inventory
decreases.
steps.expected:
Inventory updates. Vendor credit is expected on the next AP
cycle.

expected_overall: Defective material flows back to the vendor with proper paperwork.
pass_criteria: RTV created AND quarantine cleared AND vendor expected to issue credit.

title: Manual inventory adjustment with a reason code
goal:
When inventory needs adjustment outside a cycle count (damage,
shrinkage, found stock), a manual adjustment with a reason code
posts to the right variance account.
preconditions: Inventory exists for at least one part.

steps.action:
Open the inventory adjustment area. Adjust 5 ft of steel out
of stock with reason: "Damaged in handling."
steps.expected: Adjustment is accepted. Reason is required.

steps.action:
Verify GL impact: inventory account decreased, scrap / handling
variance account increased.
steps.expected: Postings target the right variance account based on the reason.

expected_overall:
Inventory adjustments are reason-coded and post to the right
variance account.
pass_criteria:
Inventory adjusted AND reason captured AND GL postings reflect the
reason.

title: Floor operator records feedback against an engineering revision
goal:
Floor operator notices an issue with a part or assembly during
production. They log feedback against the engineering revision so
R&D sees it.
preconditions: At least one BOM with a revision (P2-BOM-003).

steps.action:
At the work center, while running a WO, open the part being
produced. Find a "feedback to engineering" or "redline" action.
steps.expected: Action is available, visible to the floor.

steps.action:
Submit feedback: "Tab on bracket interferes with assembly when
bolt heads are oversized — recommend chamfer the inside corner."
Optionally attach a photo.
steps.expected: Feedback saves and routes to engineering.

steps.action:
As engineering, see the feedback in their queue, linked to the
part / revision.
steps.expected:
Feedback is visible. Engineering can acknowledge, escalate to
ECR, or close.

expected_overall:
Floor-to-engineering feedback loop works. R&D has visibility into
what's failing on the production floor.
pass_criteria:
Feedback saved AND linked to the right part / revision AND visible
to engineering.

title: Floor reports a work stoppage with reason
goal:
When work stops at a station for any reason — material shortage,
asset down, no work — the operator records why so reporting can
identify common stoppage causes.

steps.action:
At the station, choose "Report stoppage" or scan the stoppage
barcode.
steps.expected: A reason picker appears.

steps.action: Choose: "Material shortage — RM-STEEL-1018-3X3 ran out."
steps.expected: Stoppage starts. Time tracking changes from labor to stoppage.

steps.action: Once material arrives, end the stoppage and resume work.
steps.expected:
Time-on-stoppage is recorded against the WO and the reason
code. Labor resumes.

expected_overall:
Stoppages are tracked with reason codes. Reports can identify the
most common stoppage causes.
pass_criteria:
Stoppage time captured AND reason coded AND labor / stoppage time
separable in reports.

title: Inventory reconciliation as part of period close
goal:
Run an inventory reconciliation report comparing system-on-hand
to GL inventory. Investigate any variance.
preconditions: Phase 4 has produced real inventory transactions.

steps.action:
Find the inventory reconciliation report. Run as of the last
day of the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows total inventory value (system) vs. GL inventory
account balance.

steps.action:
Investigate any discrepancy. (If none — the case still passes;
that's the goal.)
steps.expected: Discrepancy explained or absent.

expected_overall: Inventory ties to GL. Any variance is identified and explainable.
pass_criteria: Reconciliation runs AND variance (if any) is explainable.

title: Take AR and AP aging snapshots at period close
goal:
Snapshot AR and AP aging at the period boundary so the historical
position is recoverable later.
preconditions: Phase 4 produced AR transactions; Phase 3 produced AP.

steps.action: Run AR aging report as of the last day of the period.
steps.expected:
Report displays buckets (current, 1-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+) and
totals per customer.

steps.action: Run AP aging report similarly.
steps.expected: AP aging displays per vendor.

steps.action: Save / archive both reports for audit purposes.
steps.expected: Both reports are stored or downloadable.

expected_overall: Period-end aging is captured.
pass_criteria:
Both reports run AND saved AND totals tie to AR and AP control
accounts on the GL.

title: Run period depreciation on fixed assets
goal:
Calculate and post the period's depreciation across all fixed
assets.
preconditions: Fixed assets exist with depreciation schedules (P3-ASSET-COMM-001).

steps.action: Open the depreciation run area. Select the period.
steps.expected:
System calculates depreciation per asset based on each asset's
schedule.

steps.action: Review the calculation. Post.
steps.expected:
Depreciation entries post: depreciation expense debited,
accumulated depreciation credited, per asset and rolled up.

expected_overall: Period depreciation is posted. Fixed asset book values reduce.
pass_criteria:
Depreciation posted AND amounts match expected straight-line
calculations AND book values updated.

title: Lock the period and prevent further postings
goal:
Once close is complete, lock the period to prevent backdated entries
that would invalidate already-published reports.
preconditions: All period adjustments and depreciation have posted.

steps.action: Find the period close action. Confirm closing.
steps.expected:
A confirmation requests the period close. After confirmation,
the period is locked.

steps.action: Try to post a journal entry dated within the closed period.
steps.expected:
System blocks the entry. The error explains the period is
closed and offers either an override (with permission) or a
route to the next open period.

steps.action:
Verify financial reports for the closed period are now
"snapshot" — running them produces the same numbers always.
steps.expected: Reports are stable.

expected_overall: Period is closed. Reports are stable. Backdated postings are blocked.
pass_criteria: Period locked AND backdated post blocked AND reports stable.
why_this_matters:
Without period close, every "month-end report" can change next time
it's run because someone backdated an entry. That destroys
accountability.

negative_variants.title: Reopen a closed period as controller
negative_variants.action:
As controller (with override authority), reopen the closed
period.
negative_variants.expected:
Reopen requires confirmation and is logged in an audit trail
(who reopened, when, why).

title: Run a routine cycle count
goal:
Perform a routine cycle count (e.g., ABC-classified parts) as part
of normal operations rather than year-end physical inventory.

steps.action:
Generate a cycle count for parts due to be counted (system
should know which based on ABC class and last count).
steps.expected: Count list is generated automatically.

steps.action: Count physically. Enter results.
steps.expected: Quantities recorded. Variances flagged.

steps.action: Approve and post adjustments for variance.
steps.expected: Inventory adjusted. Last-count date updates per part.

expected_overall: Routine cycle count runs end-to-end. ABC schedule advances.
pass_criteria: Counts captured AND adjustments posted AND last-count dates updated.

title: Process a warranty claim using a serialized product
goal:
Customer reports a warranty issue on a serialized product. The
system finds the serial, identifies the original sale and BOM
revision, and processes the claim.
preconditions: At least one serial-tracked part has been shipped (P2-PART-004, P4-SHIP).

steps.action:
In the warranty / RMA area, search for a serial number that
was shipped to a customer.
steps.expected:
System finds the serial, displays the original SO, ship date,
customer, and BOM revision in effect at production.

steps.action: Initiate a warranty claim. Capture the issue description.
steps.expected:
Claim is created and linked to the serial. The original BOM
revision is preserved as part of the claim record.

expected_overall:
Serial-traceable warranty support works end-to-end with original
production data preserved.
pass_criteria: Serial found AND original SO linked AND BOM revision captured.
why_this_matters:
Without BOM revision traceability, a warranty claim against a unit
produced 2 years ago is impossible to investigate — you don't
know which version of the design that unit was.

title: Trace all finished products built from a specific raw material lot
goal:
When a raw material lot is suspect (vendor-issued recall, internal
quality issue), find every finished good built using that lot.
preconditions: At least one lot-tracked raw material has been issued to a WO (P4-MATL-ISSUE).

steps.action: Search by the lot number "PSS-2026-04-001."
steps.expected:
System shows: original receipt, work orders that consumed it,
finished units produced, customers shipped to.

steps.action: Generate a recall report for that lot.
steps.expected: Report lists all customers and shipped quantities affected.

expected_overall:
Lot traceability holds end-to-end: raw material → WO → FG →
customer.
pass_criteria:
Lot trace produces all impacted customers AND quantities AND
serial numbers (if applicable).
why_this_matters:
This is the single most expensive thing to get wrong. A failed lot
trace turns a targeted recall into a "recall everything we shipped
in a window" — orders of magnitude more cost.

title: Ship a sub-assembly out for subcontract processing
goal:
When a routing has a subcontracted operation, the system handles
shipping out, holding while off-site, and receiving back.
preconditions: A WO is at a subcontract operation (from P2-ROUTE-002).

steps.action:
At the operation, scan to ship to the subcontractor. System
generates a shipper / pack list.
steps.expected:
Pack list and shipper are created. Material is marked as
offsite-with-vendor.

steps.action:
Verify that the material is no longer counted in available
inventory but appears in an "in-transit / offsite" report.
steps.expected: Material shows as offsite, not on-hand.

expected_overall:
Subcontracted material is properly tracked while away from the
facility.
pass_criteria:
Material flagged offsite AND shipper generated AND inventory
visibility maintained.

title: Receive material back from the subcontractor
goal:
Material returns from the subcontractor; the routing resumes from
the operation after the subcontract.

steps.action: At receiving, scan the inbound shipper from the subcontractor.
steps.expected: System matches against the offsite WO. Material is reconciled.

steps.action:
Confirm receipt. Verify the WO automatically advances past the
subcontract operation.
steps.expected: WO continues at the next routing operation.

steps.action:
The subcontract cost is recorded against the WO (per the
routing's cost from P2-ROUTE-002).
steps.expected: WO actual cost increases by the subcontract cost.

expected_overall:
Subcontract round-trip is closed. WO continues with no manual
intervention beyond the receipt.
pass_criteria: Material received AND WO advanced AND subcontract cost recorded.
